_note: Project descriptions, written to be crystal clear on the very first read: complete natural sentences, explained like you'd tell a friend, zero jargon, no 'X but for Y' fragments. Applied by scripts/final-cut.mjs. Every future addition must follow this style.
facetime-macos-ai-agent: Lets you call your own Mac on FaceTime and ask it to do things out loud. The computer actually does them for you and talks back to you during the call.
raising-cane: A white cane for blind people with a small motor inside. Instead of just warning about obstacles, it gently pulls the user toward the safest open path, the way a guide dog would, and it costs about 20 times less than products in stores.
temp-sqyptg: An online game world where your character stays alive after you log off. It learns how you talk and behave, so your friends can still chat and hang out with a version of you while you're away.
dial-4rqzc3: The team arrived at the hackathon with nowhere to sleep, so they built phone bots that called more than 100 real hotels overnight and negotiated a luxury room down to $158.
ross-42pnvi: A robot that helps blind people experience paintings. It draws the artwork on the palm of your hand, using warm and cold touches so you can feel where the different colors are.
orca-4po0nm: You hum or beatbox a melody and say which instruments you want. It turns that into a real song you can keep editing, so you can make music without knowing any music software.
dance-cv: Pick any dance video on the internet and dance along in front of your webcam. The app compares your moves to the video, gives you a score, and a voice coach tells you what to fix.
yes-or-yes: Can't make a decision? Type in your options and three live pet fish choose for you by swimming toward one. The computer then actually goes and does whatever the fish picked.
screwyouikea: Upload the instruction manual for your IKEA furniture and it becomes a 3D animation. You can spin it around and watch each step happen until the furniture is fully built.
longshot: The team typed one sentence describing Minecraft, and 200 AI assistants worked together overnight, writing code side by side until they had rebuilt the game from scratch.
shop-a-sketch: When you can't put what you want to buy into words, you just draw it. The app looks at your sketch and finds real products that look like it.
scamshield-87rbxe: A device that listens along to an elderly person's phone calls. The moment a caller sounds like a scammer, it warns them with lights and a spoken alert, then texts their family.
snowy-day-3nfbgm: After a big snowstorm, this app replans the routes snow plows take through the city, so streets get cleared faster and no neighborhood gets forgotten.
wordhawk: A small robot that plays the iPhone game Word Hunt by itself. A camera reads the letter board, the software finds the words, and a robotic finger swipes them on screen faster than any human.
diffuji: An instant camera that prints art instead of photos. You snap a picture, the camera repaints it in a dreamy art style, and it prints the result on receipt paper while you wait.
freak-cha: A test that proves you're a real human and not an AI. Instead of clicking pictures of traffic lights, you answer by sticking out your tongue in different directions, which deepfakes can't fake.
heartstart: A robot that watches for someone's heart stopping. When it happens, it drives itself across the room to the person and starts doing CPR while the ambulance is on the way.
lifesaver-lnm0tz: Lets medical students practice surgery like a video game. The webcam tracks their hands while a voice guides them through each step, and they get a score at the end, with no real patient needed.
kinemo: A party game console that needs no controllers at all. The webcam watches your body, so you jump, duck, and wave your arms to play the games.
artificial-sandwich-intelligence: A robot that learned to make sandwiches just by watching people make them for four hours. It was built from robot arms that cost only $200.
clipper-ph49un: An app that keeps you honest while studying. If you quit your study session early, it takes a photo of you and sends it to your friends so they know you gave up.
wing-cet3o0: A safety app for people living with an abusive partner. It looks and works exactly like a normal weather app, so if the abuser checks the phone, nothing seems unusual.
ted-ai-x8537t: A teddy bear that can tell how you're feeling when you hug it, using your heartbeat and how tightly you squeeze. It talks back gently, like a friend who always knows when something's wrong.
maestro-n0uqyz: Point a camera at a broom and play it like a real guitar. It tracks your hands and turns air-strumming into actual notes and chords, and whatever you play can grow into a fully produced song.
viniclip: Press record and just talk. If you stumble, pause, or restart a sentence, the app cuts those parts out while you're still recording, so the moment you stop you have a clean, finished video ready to post.
plant-hopper: A robotic water-gun turret for your houseplants. It looks around the room, figures out which plants are thirsty, then aims and squirts each one with exactly the water it needs.
chessmate-nwygvq: A real chessboard where the pieces slide across the board by themselves, moved by a magnet underneath. You play against family on the other side of the world, and their moves appear on your board like magic.
readable-ai-braille-printer: Braille printers normally cost about $1,500, so this team built one for under $15 from a cheap 3D printer. Feed it any document and it punches the whole thing out in braille, even describing the pictures in plain words.
fable-br8e1d: You wear a headband that reads your brainwaves while a story unfolds on screen. The plot, pictures, and sound change depending on how focused or relaxed you actually are, so no two people ever experience the same story.
vibe-director: A tiny 3D movie studio you walk around in, staffed by virtual workers. You chat with the director about the story, the writer about dialogue, and the artist about your cast, and out comes a finished short film.
signhero: A rhythm game that teaches you sign language. Letters scroll down the screen like falling notes, and you score points by making each hand sign in time with the music while your webcam checks you got it right.
s-kbd67: They hacked a Nerf gun into a video game controller. Pulling the trigger shoots in the game, pumping it reloads, and pointing it aims, so you play shooter games with a real toy gun in your hands.
rehabify-y2f5mu: Doing physical therapy at home, you never know if you're doing the exercises right. This app watches through your webcam and corrects you out loud the moment your form slips, like having a therapist in the room.
reeljobs: Instead of reading walls of text on job boards, you swipe through a feed of short videos where every job listing has been turned into a 20-second clip. When you see one you like, one tap fills out the application for you.
the-unspillable: A spoon that keeps its bowl perfectly level no matter how much your hand shakes, so people with tremors can eat without spilling. The real product costs over $300; this team built one in a weekend with a tiny computer chip and two small motors.
project-horizon-8iodp2: A soccer game where you can watch the match through the eyes of any player on the field. Instead of looking down from above, you become the striker or the keeper and experience the game as them.
coach-bob: A punching bag with a personality. Through AR glasses, glowing targets light up on the bag showing where to punch, it measures how hard you hit, and Bob trash-talks you to keep you moving.
wateryourdoin: Every question you ask an AI uses real water to cool the computers that answer it. This browser add-on catches questions a normal search could answer, nudges you to skip them, and shows the water you saved as little ice cubes.
vril: Type a description of any object and it becomes a real 3D model you can tweak and send straight to a 3D printer, no design software needed.
digital-physical-darts-wii-darts: They built a real dart with a motion sensor inside, so you swing your arm like you're actually throwing and the dart flies across a dartboard on screen. It's like building your own Wii controller from scratch.
blinkai: For people who can't move or speak, this app turns blinking into a voice. You blink short and long like Morse code, and it speaks full sentences out loud for you.
project-lend: A food bank with no people in it. Donors text in what they have, a robot arm sorts the food into boxes, and AI helpers arrange the pickups. Over one weekend it delivered about 100 pounds of food to real shelters.
ghostdiedie: A 3D fighting game where you and a friend battle by physically moving in front of your webcams. You see each other's real faces on the fighters while you dodge and swing.
lume-zrlypk: A patient voice helper for grandparents using a computer. Ask it how to unmute on Zoom and it draws a glowing highlight right on the button to click, like a tech-savvy grandkid who is always around.
replay-wq5isz: Record a real room with a phone and two small sensors, then replay the moment in 3D. You can pause, rewind, and fly the camera anywhere inside the memory, like a video game replay of real life.
taqr: Portable laser tag where you reload by picking up real litter and scanning it into the game. Cleaning the park stops being a chore because every piece of trash powers your next round.
dose-ebmo9z: A pill bottle that weighs itself to know whether grandma took her medicine today. If a dose is missed, the family gets a message before it becomes a problem.
whalebeing-27sj0w: Cargo ships accidentally hit and kill blue whales on busy routes. This app redraws shipping routes around the places whales feed and travel, so ships lose barely any time and whales keep their lives.
archemy: A crafting game pulled into the real world. Through AR glasses you grab floating objects with your bare hands and smash them together to invent new ones, so a duck plus a car becomes a quackmobile.
resqme-jq52al: When a disaster knocks out cell towers and wifi, these cheap pocket radios keep working. They pass your texts and location from one device to the next until they reach rescuers.
